Metric: Signs covering 75 percent of analyzable tokens
Interpretation: Compatible with a phonetic core plus extensions; not diagnostic by itself.
Large carrier set supports testing a phonetic or morphemic core, while marker signs must be modeled separately.
A substantial marker set would push the system toward administrative formula grammar rather than a plain alphabetic inventory.

Metric: Signs covering 90 percent of analyzable tokens
Interpretation: This is the practical modeling target before the sparse tail dominates uncertainty.
The long tail is compatible with variants, numbers, names, object marks, rare logograms, or catalog over-splitting; it should not drive early decipherment.

Metric: Tail signs with dominant left/right neighbor
Interpretation: These are computational leads for diacritic, conjunct, affix, or formula-variant behavior.
